item.title: 全球经济图解：伊朗战争爆发第二周，油价突破100美元
item.description: 美国表示，随着席卷中东的战争进入第两周，并继续扰乱能源供应和全球市场，美方已将对伊朗的空袭升级至前所未有的程度。【原文：The US said it had stepped up strikes on Iran to unprecedented levels as the war that’s engulfed the Middle East hit the two-week mark and continued to upend energy flows and global markets.】

item.title: 2026年前两个月，中国国家电网投资大幅增长
item.description: 中国国家电网公司表示，随着北京方面着力稳定经济增长并扩大基础设施支出，该公司正加快对电网的投资。【原文：State Grid Corp. of China said it’s accelerating investment in power grids as Beijing pushes to stabilize growth and expand infrastructure spending.】

item.title: 富查伊拉部分石油作业因无人机袭击及火灾暂停
item.description: 据知情人士透露，位于霍尔木兹海峡入口处的富查伊拉港，部分装油作业因周六上午发生的无人机袭击及火灾而暂停。【原文：Some oil loading operations in the port of Fujairah, just outside the Strait of Hormuz, have been suspended after a drone attack and fire on Saturday morning, people familiar with the matter said.】

item.title: 中国游客在春假期间出游人数创下新高
item.description: 新华社周六报道称，春节期间，中国游客的国内出游人数创下历史新高。【原文：Chinese travelers took a record number of domestic trips during the Spring Festival Travel season, Xinhua News Agency reported Saturday.】

item.title: 两艘液化石油气船驶过霍尔木兹海峡，前往面临供应短缺的印度
item.description: 两艘载有液化石油气的油轮在穿越霍尔木兹海峡后正驶往印度，这在一定程度上缓解了因波斯湾战争导致烹饪燃料供应中断而造成的严重短缺。【原文：Two tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas are heading to India after crossing the Strait of Hormuz, providing some relief to acute shortages as the war in the Persian Gulf disrupts supply of the cooking fuel.】

item.title: JSW钢铁公司获得莫桑比克一座炼焦煤矿的开发权
item.description: 印度产能最大的钢铁制造商JSW钢铁有限公司赢得了在莫桑比克特特省开发一座煤矿的权利，从而确保了钢铁生产所需的关键原材料的供应。【原文：JSW Steel Ltd., India’s biggest steelmaker by capacity, won rights to develop a coal mine in Mozambique’s Tete province, securing access to a key raw material used in steel manufacturing.】

item.title: 瑞士法郎势不可挡，给出口商带来新的痛苦
item.description: 瑞士企业家克里斯蒂安·坦勒很早就意识到，他必须让自己的企业做好应对瑞郎升值的准备。【原文：Swiss entrepreneur Christian Taennler knew early on that he had to get his business ready for a stronger franc.】

item.title: 俄罗斯称大型炼油厂和重要港口遭乌克兰无人机袭击
item.description: 俄罗斯当局表示，乌克兰无人机袭击引发了一家大型炼油厂起火，并袭击了俄罗斯克拉斯诺达尔边疆区的一个重要港口。【原文：Ukrainian drone attacks sparked a fire at a major oil refinery and struck a key port in Russia’s Krasnodar region, Russian authorities said.】

item.title: 美国袭击哈尔格岛军事设施，伊朗再次发出报复威胁
item.description: 伊朗警告称，如果其基础设施遭到袭击，将对中东地区与美国有关联的石油和能源设施发动袭击。此前，美国轰炸了哈格岛这一关键前哨基地上的军事目标，伊朗再次重申了这一威胁。【原文：Iran warned it will target American-linked oil and energy facilities in the Middle East if its own infrastructure is attacked, reiterating its threat after the US bombed military targets on the critical outpost of Kharg Island.】

item.title: 浙江与吉利深化合作，共建世界级汽车产业集群
item.description: 中国浙江省政府与吉利汽车集团签署了一项新的战略合作协议，双方由此加深了合作关系，该地区正致力于巩固其在全球电动汽车供应链中的地位。【原文：China’s Zhejiang provincial government signed a new strategic cooperation agreement with automobile group Geely, deepening ties as the region seeks to strengthen its position in the global electric‑vehicle supply chain.】

item.title: 澳大利亚农村地区面临“无法接受”的燃油短缺
item.description: 澳大利亚能源部长克里斯·鲍文表示，农村和地区正面临“真实且无法接受的”燃料短缺，这是美以与伊朗冲突引发的连锁反应，该冲突导致全球油价飙升。【原文：Australia’s Energy Minister Chris Bowen says rural and regional areas are facing “real and unacceptable shortages” of fuel, a ripple effect of the US-Israel war with Iran that has caused global oil prices to surge.】

item.title: 伊朗石油枢纽遭袭引发对供应进一步动荡的担忧
item.description: 美国对该岛发动的大规模袭击——该岛出口了伊朗大部分原油——引发了人们对该地区供应中断范围可能进一步扩大的担忧，这给中东地区经历两周战乱后本已动荡不安的石油和天然气市场带来了更大压力。【原文：A major US attack on the island that exports the bulk of Iran’s crude is raising fears of more widespread supply disruptions in the region, further straining oil and gas markets already in upheaval after two weeks of war in the Middle East.】

item.title: GE Vernova与日立将共同在东南亚探索小型反应堆项目
item.description: GE Vernova公司与日立公司达成协议，将共同探讨在东南亚部署小型模块化核反应堆的机遇。【原文：GE Vernova Inc. and Hitachi Ltd. agreed to explore opportunities to deploy small modular nuclear reactors in Southeast Asia.】

item.title: 美国开始释放8600万桶战略石油储备
item.description: 特朗普政府启动了大规模动用美国战略石油储备的程序，并提出动用8600万桶原油的请求。【原文：The Trump administration started the process of a mammoth drawdown of the US emergency oil reserve, issuing a request to exchange 86 million barrels of crude oil.】

item.title: 美国撤回要求对全球人工智能芯片实施许可的规则草案
item.description: 据政府网站上发布的一份电子通知显示，美国商务部已撤回了一项拟议法规，该法规原本将限制未经美国批准向全球任何地区出口人工智能芯片。【原文：The US Commerce Department has pulled a draft regulation that would have restricted exports of artificial intelligence chips to anywhere in the world without US approval, according to an electronic notification posted on a government website.】

item.title: 战争引发的燃料短缺开始威胁全球粮食供应
item.description: 粮食作物正日益受到中东战争引发的能源供应短缺的影响，亚洲和欧洲各地的农民都面临着用于操作必要机械的燃料短缺问题。【原文：Food crops are becoming increasingly vulnerable to the energy supply crunch caused by war in the Middle East, with farmers across Asia and Europe facing a scarcity of fuel needed to operate essential machinery.】

item.title: 在OpenAI欺诈案的庭审中，无法对埃隆·马斯克使用氯胺酮一事进行调查
item.description: 在即将举行的陪审团审判中，OpenAI公司及其首席执行官萨姆·阿尔特曼的律师将无法就埃隆·马斯克使用麻醉剂氯胺酮一事进行质询。此次审判涉及对这家生成式人工智能公司因背离其非营利初衷而欺诈马斯克的指控。【原文：Elon Musk’s use of the drug ketamine will be off limits to attorneys for OpenAI Inc. and its chief executive officer Sam Altman during an upcoming jury trial over claims that the generative AI company defrauded Musk by abandoning its nonprofit roots.】

item.title: 特朗普政府敲定与厄瓜多尔的贸易协议，以削减关税
item.description: 美国贸易代表杰米森·格里尔周五签署了一项协议，正式敲定了与厄瓜多尔的贸易协定，降低了两国间部分商品的关税税率。【原文：US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer on Friday signed an agreement finalizing a trade deal with Ecuador, reducing tariff rates on a number of goods traded between the two economies.】

item.title: 《华尔街日报》报道：美国将因TikTok交易获得100亿美元费用
item.description: 据《华尔街日报》周五报道，作为中国字节跳动公司将短视频应用TikTok的美国业务出售给美国投资者财团的交易的一部分，美国预计将从投资者处获得约100亿美元的费用。【原文：The US is slated to receive around $10 billion as a fee from investors as part of the recently completed deal for China’s ByteDance Ltd. to sell the American operations of the social video app TikTok to a consortium of US investors, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday.】

item.title: 蓝猫私募信贷BDC敦促拒绝博阿兹·温斯坦的收购要约
item.description: 蓝猫头鹰资本公司（Blue Owl Capital Inc.）旗下的一只基金正敦促投资者拒绝由博阿兹·温斯坦（Boaz Weinstein）旗下的萨巴资本管理公司（Saba Capital Management）牵头的股份收购要约，称该要约价格过低。【原文：A Blue Owl Capital Inc. fund is urging investors to reject a share purchase offer led by Boaz Weinstein’s Saba Capital Management, saying the offer price is too low.】

item.title: 道明银行表示，企业债券目前价格已足够低廉，值得考虑买入
item.description: 道明证券认为，现在正是投资者考虑承担更多风险、买入公司债券的好时机。【原文：TD Securities believes now is the time for investors to consider taking on a little more risk and buying corporate bonds.】

item.title: 对数字经济征税的呼声日益高涨
item.description:
在今天的《Big Take》播客中：数字服务已成为国际贸易中增长最快的领域——部分原因在于它们不受全球关税的限制。但这种情况直到现在才改变。【原文：

On today’s Big Take podcast: Digital services have become the fastest-growing segment of international trade — in part because they’re unencumbered by global tariffs. Until now.

】

item.title: 伦敦证券交易所集团计划下周发行最高达30亿美元的债券
item.description: 据知情人士透露，伦敦证券交易所集团（London Stock Exchange Group Plc）计划最早于下周初发行高达30亿美元的高评级美国企业债券。【原文：The London Stock Exchange Group Plc is seeking to sell as much as $3 billion of high-grade US corporate bonds as soon as early next week, according to people with knowledge of the transaction.】

item.title: 橡树资本的波利表示，信贷市场将出现更大的价格波动
item.description: 橡树资本管理公司的丹妮尔·波利表示，鉴于全球信贷市场仍相对稳健，这家专注于不良债务的投资机构大规模收购该类资产的时机尚未到来。【原文：Oaktree Capital Management’s Danielle Poli says the moment for the distressed debt shop to scoop up the asset class in droves isn’t here yet as global credit markets remain relatively resilient.】

item.title: 随着伊朗局势紧张，对冲基金对原油的看涨情绪升至2020年以来最高水平
item.description: 在原油市场迎来有史以来波动最剧烈的一周之初，对冲基金对布伦特原油的看涨情绪达到了六年来的最高点。【原文：Hedge funds turned the most bullish on Brent oil in six years at the outset of one of the crude market’s most volatile weeks ever.】

item.title: 美国允许委内瑞拉出售化肥，因战争导致供应紧张
item.description: 特朗普政府批准委内瑞拉向美国公司出售化肥及其他石化产品，就在伊朗局势导致全球关键作物营养素供应趋紧之际，此举进一步放宽了制裁。【原文：The Trump administration authorized Venezuela to sell fertilizers and other petrochemicals to US companies, further loosening sanctions just as the Iran war tightens global supplies of critical crop nutrients.】

item.title: “2秒内损失100万美元”：市场动荡冲击交易台
item.description: 伦敦时间凌晨1点，市场动荡将迈克尔·布朗惊醒。他的手机就放在床边，此刻正不断发出提示音，简直是“嗡嗡嗡”地响个不停：布伦特原油突破每桶100美元；超过110美元；纳斯达克期货下跌2%；日经指数暴跌5%。【原文：It was 1 a.m. in London when the markets jarred Michael Brown awake. He had his phone next to his bed, and it had started pinging him with alerts, just “buzzing and buzzing and buzzing”: Brent crude is over $100 a barrel; over $110; Nasdaq futures sink 2%; the Nikkei plunges 5%.】

item.title: 特拉维斯·卡兰尼克推出“有偿就业机器人”计划
item.description: 优步科技公司（Uber Technologies Inc.）联合创始人特拉维斯·卡兰尼克（Travis Kalanick）启动了一项新业务，致力于为餐饮、采矿和运输行业打造“能创造经济价值的机器人”。【原文：Uber Technologies Inc. co-founder Travis Kalanick has launched a new venture that will focus on creating “gainfully employed robots” for the food, mining and transport industries.】

item.title: 布伦特原油连续第二个交易日收于100美元上方
item.description: 受中东冲突持续发酵以及世界各国领导人竭力应对石油市场历史上最大规模的供应中断影响，布伦特原油连续第二个交易日收于每桶100美元上方，当日收盘价创下逾三年来的最高水平。【原文：Brent crude settled above $100 a barrel for the second straight session, ending the day at the highest level in more than three years as the conflict in the Middle East drags on and world leaders struggle to resolve the biggest disruption to the oil market in history.】

item.title: 随着交易商担忧霍尔木兹海峡封锁将持续更久，石油危机进一步加剧
item.description: 石油市场历史上最严重的供应中断目前仍无缓解迹象，自伊朗冲突爆发以来，原油价格已飙升40%，这让全球经济难以喘息。【原文：The worst supply disruption in the history of the oil market is showing no signs of abating anytime soon, offering the global economy little respite from crude prices that have surged 40% since the Iran war began.】
